name: Donna
notes: NYC's first worker-owned cocktail bar. USFWC member. Pure worker co-op.

name: BOYFRIEND co-op
notes: Worker-owned cooperative cafe and cocktail bar. Queer-focused.

name: Workers Tap & Cafe
notes: Worker-owned beer bar. Profits split equally among members.

name: Democracy Brewing
notes: Boston's first worker-owned brewery and pub. USFWC member.

name: Democracy Brewing East Boston
notes: Second location of Democracy Brewing, opened 2025.

name: Conor Byrne Pub
notes: FLAG: Hybrid worker + ~600 community member co-op. Employees led buyout in 2024.

name: New Helvetia Brewing Company
notes: Multi-stakeholder co-op with Workers Assembly. CA's first cooperatively owned commercial brewery.

name: The Grain Shed
notes: Worker and farmer-owned cooperative. Brewery, bakery, and taproom.

name: Broken Clock Brewing Cooperative
notes: FLAG: Member co-op — workers can become owner-members but public can also join.

name: Wildland Cooperative
location: 8022 E Green Bluff Rd, Colbert, WA 99005
notes: FLAG: Worker-owned brewery and winery taproom on farm property. Colbert is rural, ~15 miles north of Spokane.

name: Bathtub Row Brewing Co-op
location: 163 Central Park Square, Los Alamos, NM 87544
notes: Hybrid consumer and worker co-op brewery.

name: The Stud
notes: First worker-owned cooperative nightclub in the US. LGBTQ+ bar. Reopened at new location April 2024.

name: Full Barrel Cooperative Brewery
notes: FLAG: Consumer/member co-op, not worker co-op. Vermont's first community-owned brewery. Nanobrewery, open Fri-Sat only.

name: Ronan Cooperative Brewery
notes: FLAG: Consumer/member co-op (shares at $250). Montana's first cooperative brewery. Opened September 2020.

name: Flying Bike Cooperative Brewery
notes: FLAG: Consumer/member co-op (public membership at $200). Washington's first cooperatively owned brewery. Founded 2011.

name: New Barons Brewing Cooperative
notes: FLAG: Consumer/member co-op (~650 members at $200). Wisconsin's first cooperative brewery. Operates as a brewery incubator.
